target.title: fix(together, pixverse): bound video response reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Together and PixVerse success-path control JSON responses are read from external provider endpoints. On main, the affected Together create-video and PixVerse upload/create paths can still reach unbounded `response.json()` reads, so a buggy or hostile endpoint that streams a very large JSON body with no `Content-Length` can force the runtime to buffer the whole response before parsing it.

This PR caps those success-path JSON reads with the existing shared 16 MiB provider JSON reader, matching the bounded-response-read pattern used by the sibling provider hardening PRs.

## Changes

- `extensions/together/video-generation-provider.ts`: route the create-video success JSON through `readProviderJsonResponse(response, "Together video generation failed")` via `readTogetherVideoJson`.
- `extensions/pixverse/video-generation-provider.ts`: route `readPixVerseJson` through `readProviderJsonResponse` before the existing PixVerse envelope validation.
- `extensions/together/video-generation-provider.test.ts`: keep provider HTTP mocks on real streamed `Response` bodies and add an oversized create-response regression.
- `extensions/pixverse/video-generation-provider.test.ts`: keep PixVerse success mocks on real streamed `Response` bodies and add oversized upload/create regressions.

## Real Behavior Proof

Current PR head: `ee3dbd80721fd969ac25c7b4d3c491444afd6554`.

A real `node:http` server bound to `127.0.0.1` streamed oversized JSON bodies in 64 KiB chunks without `Content-Length`. The proof drove current-head replicas of the exact success-path wrappers: Together uses the real shared `readProviderJsonResponse` label `Together video generation failed`, and PixVerse uses the same shared bounded reader plus local envelope validation. A negative control used an old-style unbounded read against the same streaming body.

```text
$ node --import tsx scratchpad/together-pixverse-bound-proof.mts
[proof] real node:http server on http://127.0.0.1:33477, cap=16777216 bytes, would-stream≈67108864 bytes per overflow route
PASS  Together create JSON: overflow throws bounded error :: threw=true msg="Together video generation failed: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes"
PASS  Together create JSON: stream cancelled near 16MiB :: aborted=true bytesSent=19660807 (<33554432)
PASS  PixVerse JSON: overflow throws bounded error :: threw=true msg="PixVerse video generation failed: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes"
PASS  PixVerse JSON: stream cancelled near 16MiB :: aborted=true bytesSent=19660807 (<33554432)
PASS  negative control: OLD unbounded read buffers PAST the 16MiB cap :: buffered=67108871 bytes (> 16777216)
PASS  negative control consumed far more than bounded reads :: unbounded buffered=67108871 vs Together sent≈19660807
PASS  Together happy path: small JSON still parses :: id=video_happy status=completed
PASS  PixVerse happy path: small enveloped JSON still parses :: video_id=123

[proof] 8 PASS / 0 FAIL
[proof] ALL PASS
```

Focused tests against the current PR head:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/together/video-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/pixverse/video-generation-provider.test.ts -- --run

Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  17 passed (17)

Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  8 passed (8)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 59.72s
```

## Compatibility / Risk

This intentionally changes oversized Together/PixVerse success control JSON from unbounded buffering to fail-closed at the shared 16 MiB provider JSON cap. Normal small JSON responses continue to parse, and PixVerse envelope behavior remains local. I did not call the live provider services because live endpoints are not suitable for reproducing hostile oversized streaming bodies.

**Label**: security

_AI-assisted._
target.author: alix-007
